content:
# Food Safety Document Inventory Checklist

**Tool ID:** T016-01
**Block:** B016 (Food Safety Record-Keeping)
**Domain:** Food Safety & Hygiene (D01_food_safety)
**Level:** L1
**Format:** Checklist
**Role:** Manager
**Time Required:** 10-15 minutes
**Version:** 1.0

---
## Welcome

This is the foundational record-keeping tool that stops VMVT inspectors finding gaps before you do. Every document on this list has been requested during a real inspection -- if it is missing or expired, you lose points.

Not another tick-box exercise. This checklist exists because the details it covers are the ones that slip when you are busy -- and the ones that matter most when they do.

**Built for Manager working in Food Safety & Hygiene.**
Use it. Adapt it. Make it yours.

---

## Purpose

Complete inventory of all required food safety documents, showing what should be in the file, its current status (present/missing/out of date), and the action needed. Run this monthly or whenever a document is updated. Keep completed copies in your food safety folder -- inspectors ask to see them.

This tool supports **Food Safety Record-Keeping** (B016) and is designed to be practical, printable, and immediately useful in a working cafe environment.

---

## How to Use This Tool

**Who:** Manager
**Time Required:** 10-15 minutes
**Frequency:** Monthly, plus after any document change (new supplier, new staff member, certificate renewal).

**What You Will Need:**
- This checklist (printed or tablet)
- Access to the food safety folder / binder
- Pen or digital annotation tool
- Contact details for suppliers and training providers

**Steps:**
1. Gather your food safety folder, staff files, and supplier records
2. Work through each section below, checking every document against the original
3. Mark status: PRESENT / MISSING / EXPIRED / NEEDS UPDATE
4. For any item not PRESENT, record the corrective action and deadline
5. File the completed checklist at the front of your food safety folder
6. Follow up on all actions within the stated deadlines

---

## Checklist

### Section A -- Core Regulatory Documents

| # | Document | Status | Expiry / Review Date | Action Required | Deadline | Initials |
|---|----------|--------|---------------------|-----------------|----------|----------|
| A1 | Food business registration confirmation (VMVT) | P / M | N/A -- permanent | | | |
| A2 | Current food hygiene rating certificate (displayed publicly) | P / M | Last inspection: _____ | | | |
| A3 | HACCP plan / food safety management system documentation | P / M / U | Review due: _____ | | | |
| A4 | Allergen matrix -- current menu (all 14 EU allergens) | P / M / U | Last updated: _____ | | | |
| A5 | Water supply compliance certificate (if private supply) | P / N/A | Expiry: _____ | | | |
| A6 | Pest control contract and latest report | P / M | Last visit: _____ | | | |
| A7 | Public liability insurance certificate | P / M | Expiry: _____ | | | |

*Status key: P = Present, M = Missing, U = Needs Update, N/A = Not Applicable*

### Section B -- Daily / Weekly Operational Records

| # | Document | Status | Location | Gaps Found? | Action Required | Deadline | Initials |
|---|----------|--------|----------|-------------|-----------------|----------|----------|
| B1 | Fridge temperature log (daily, target 1-5 deg C) | P / M / G | | Yes / No | | | |
| B2 | Freezer temperature log (daily, target -18 deg C or below) | P / M / G | | Yes / No | | | |
| B3 | Hot-hold temperature log (63 deg C+ where applicable) | P / M / N/A | | Yes / No | | | |
| B4 | Cooking temperature log (core temp 75 deg C+) | P / M / G | | Yes / No | | | |
| B5 | Probe thermometer calibration record | P / M | | Yes / No | | | |
| B6 | Delivery acceptance records (temp checks, date codes) | P / M / G | | Yes / No | | | |
| B7 | Cleaning schedule and completed cleaning records | P / M / G | | Yes / No | | | |
| B8 | Food waste / disposal log | P / M / G | | Yes / No | | | |

*Status key: P = Present, M = Missing, G = Gaps in records*

### Section C -- Staff Records

| # | Document | Status | Missing For | Action Required | Deadline | Initials |
|---|----------|--------|-------------|-----------------|----------|----------|
| C1 | Staff food hygiene certificates (Level 2 minimum per person) | All / Gaps | _____ | | | |
| C2 | Allergen training completion records | All / Gaps | _____ | | | |
| C3 | Food safety induction sign-off sheets | All / Gaps | _____ | | | |
| C4 | Fitness-to-work / return-from-illness declarations | All / Gaps | _____ | | | |
| C5 | Personal hygiene policy acknowledgement | All / Gaps | _____ | | | |

### Section D -- Supplier and Traceability Documents

| # | Document | Status | Missing For | Action Required | Deadline | Initials |
|---|----------|--------|-------------|-----------------|----------|----------|
| D1 | Approved supplier list with contact details | P / M / U | | | | |
| D2 | Supplier food safety certificates / audits | All / Gaps | _____ | | | |
| D3 | Allergen information from suppliers (spec sheets) | All / Gaps | _____ | | | |
| D4 | Delivery notes / invoices (traceability trail, last 3 months) | P / Gaps | | | | |

### Section E -- Corrective Actions and Incident Records

| # | Document | Status | Notes | Action Required | Deadline | Initials |
|---|----------|--------|-------|-----------------|----------|----------|
| E1 | Corrective action log (temperature breaches, complaints) | P / M | | | | |
| E2 | Customer complaint records (food safety related) | P / M / None | | | | |
| E3 | Food recall procedure document | P / M | | | | |
| E4 | Previous inspection reports and action plans | P / M | | | | |

### Summary

| Section | Total Items | Present | Missing / Expired | Actions Needed |
|---------|------------|---------|-------------------|----------------|
| A -- Regulatory | 7 | | | |
| B -- Operational Records | 8 | | | |
| C -- Staff Records | 5 | | | |
| D -- Supplier / Traceability | 4 | | | |
| E -- Corrective Actions | 4 | | | |
| **TOTAL** | **28** | | | |

**Completion:**
- Date: _______________
- Completed by: _______________
- Reviewed by: _______________
- Next review due: _______________
- Follow-up needed: Yes / No

**If any item is MISSING or EXPIRED:**
Set a corrective deadline (maximum 7 days for critical items A1-A4, 14 days for all others). Log the action in your corrective action record (E1). Escalate to Owner if more than 3 items are non-compliant.

---

## Common Risks

### Risk 1: Allergen Matrix Not Updated After Menu Change

**Why it happens:** New dish added to the specials board, but nobody updates the allergen matrix. Inspector asks for proof, and the matrix does not match the current menu.
**How to prevent:** Make allergen matrix review a mandatory step in any menu change process. Use T016-04 retention schedule as a trigger.
**If it is already happening:** Pull your current menu right now, compare it line-by-line with the allergen matrix, and update immediately.

### Risk 2: Staff Certificates Missing for Recent Hires

**Why it happens:** New starter begins work, food hygiene course is booked for next month, but no temporary record is created. VMVT inspector asks to see certificates for all staff on shift.
**How to prevent:** Require Level 2 certificate (or proof of booking with completion date) before the first solo shift. Track in T019-01.
**If it is already happening:** Check every person currently on your rota against section C. Book missing training this week.

### Risk 3: Temperature Logs Have Gaps on Weekends

**Why it happens:** Weekend shifts are staffed differently. The person responsible for logging is not briefed. Monday manager finds blank rows.
**How to prevent:** Assign temperature logging to a specific role (opening barista), not a specific person. Include it in the opening checklist T092-01.
**If it is already happening:** Back-fill what you can from probe thermometer memory. Add a note explaining the gap and the corrective action taken.

---

## Customisation Notes

**This tool is a starting point, not a rulebook.**

**Independent / Specialty Cafe:** Add sections for specialty items -- single-origin coffee traceability, raw milk documentation, or artisan supplier certifications.
**Chain / Franchise:** Map each item to your brand compliance checklist. Use this as a cross-reference to ensure nothing falls between corporate and local requirements.
**Kiosk / Mobile:** Sections D and E can be simplified. Focus on A1-A4 and B1-B7 as your minimum viable document set.
**Bakery Cafe:** Add proving temperature records, flour traceability, and baked goods shelf-life documentation.

**Making it yours:**
- Cross out sections that do not apply to your venue
- Add any site-specific permits (alcohol licence, outdoor seating permit)
- Adjust frequency based on inspection feedback
- Share the completed summary with your Owner quarterly

The structure matters more than the specific fields. Keep the structure, change the content.

---

## Connected Resources

**Other tools in this block:**
- T016-02 -- Monthly Record Review Checklist (L2, checklist)
- T016-03 -- Food Safety Record-Keeping SOP (L2, SOP)
- T016-04 -- Record Retention Schedule (L2, template)
- T016-05 -- Quarterly Record Completeness Audit (L3, audit)

**Related blocks:**
- B013
- B014

**Parent block:** B016 -- Food Safety Record-Keeping
**Module:** M01: Food Safety & Hygiene Compliance
---

*Tool Version 1.0 -- OIS Cafe v2*
*Built for cafe operators who value clarity, consistency, and craft.*
